channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Now that they've passed a budget plan, the hard part begins for Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans narrowly got their budget plan over the finish line. Now comes the hard part. The resolution adopted this week was only a first step that allows Republicans to draft legislation that they can push through Congress without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In fight over insurance, neighbors crowdsource LA fire contamination data
item.description: All sense of survivors’ guilt was fleeting for those residents whose homes remained standing after wildfires ripped through the Los Angeles area three months ago.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER and SALLY HO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration to refer Maine to Justice Department over transgender participation in sports
item.description: The deadline has arrived for Maine officials to reach a resolution with the U.S. Education Department over a finding that the state violated antidiscrimination laws by allowing transgender athletes to participate in girls’ sports.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pro-Palestinian demonstrators who occupied Stanford building are charged with felonies
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — A dozen pro-Palestinian demonstrators who were arrested at Stanford University last year after they occupied and allegedly caused hundreds of thousands of dollars in damage to a campus building are now facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, KREX 5

item.title: Judge will halt Trump administration from ending humanitarian parole for people from four countries
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she will prevent the Trump administration from ordering hundreds of thousands of Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans with temporary legal status to leave the country later this month.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democratic prosecutor in crime-torn Albuquerque launches bid for New Mexico governor
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A Democratic district attorney in the eye of New Mexico’s tempest over crime and accountability launched his candidacy for governor Thursday as the National Guard prepares to deploy to the state’s largest city to shore up public safety.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pressed for evidence against Mahmoud Khalil, government cites its power to deport people for beliefs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Facing a deadline from an immigration judge to turn over evidence for its attempted deportation of Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil, the federal government has instead submitted a brief memo, signed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, citing the Trump administration’s autho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Six people have died in a helicopter crash in the Hudson River in New York City, an AP source says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six people have died in a helicopter crash in the Hudson River in New York City, an Associated Press source says. THIS IS A BREAKING NEWS UPDATE. AP’s earlier story follows below.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as regulations issued under the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homeland Security agents denied entry at two Los Angeles schools
item.description: It is believed to be the first time federal agents tried to enter Los Angeles schools since President Trump announced a crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Accused Austin Metcalf killer won't face death penalty or life without parole: DA
item.description: The teen accused of stabbing track star Austin Metcalf to death will not face life behind bars or the death penalty, according to a Texas district attorney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: International students are seeing their visas revoked for infractions as minor as traffic violations.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Playground demolition conversation evolves into how to address homelessness
item.description: "If I had a child I would not let them play there because they might find hypodermic needles or something else," said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Paul Steeno, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Brawl erupts between college baseball teams
item.description: A fight broke out Tuesday night during a game between the Spring Hill College and University of Mobile baseball teams.
item.dc:creator: Summer Poole, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Johnson presses ahead with vote on Trump budget blueprint
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said he was pressing forward with a vote Thursday morning on the party's “one big, beautiful bill” full of President Trump’s legislative agenda, holding a joint appearance with Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Texas lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Court of Appeals rejects petition to dismiss indictment of ex-congressional candidate accused of murder
item.description: The Nevada Court of Appeals Wednesday rejected a petition to dismiss the indictment against a former Republican congressional candidate and ex-wrestler charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State leaders tell Trump cabinet members that Utah should run its own federal programs
item.description: Utah's Speaker of the House Mike Schultz is among the top state leaders pushing for Utah to have control over federal programs.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Aerts, ABC 4

item.title: Four Americans accused of Congo coup attempt now facing federal charges in Salt Lake City
item.description: Four Americans accused of participating in a failed coup attempt in the Democratic Republic of Congo will now go to trial in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: NYC lets immigration officials open an office at Rikers jail, a priority for Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams ' administration is allowing federal immigration officials to operate at Rikers Island to work on gang and drug-related criminal investigations in the city's largest lockup.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The world's most famous YouTuber slams Trump's tariff war
item.description: Entrepreneur and YouTube star MrBeast slammed President Trump’s “brutal” tariffs in a post on the social platform X on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Group calls for investigation into Ms. Rachel over posts about Gaza children
item.description: Rachel Griffin Accurso, who goes by Ms. Rachel to her more than 14 million subscribers on YouTube, makes videos for children.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WGN 9

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Democratic-led cities and states push back on threats to cut US school funding over DEI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Some Democratic-led states and cities are pushing back on a Trump administration threat to cut education funding over diversity, equity and inclusion programs, creating a standoff that could test how far the White House is willing to go to press its demands on the nation’s schools.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, WGN 9
